value: Upgrade your weapons.
Use bonus actions to your advantage.
Aim for a Support Attack.
Protect allies with a Support Defense.
Give SP conversion a try.
Give scouting a try.
Give dismantlement a try.
Aim for Development Diagram completion.
Take on secret battles.
Try saving formations.
Take advantage of the Warship Cruise.
Take advantage of character requests.
Always be mindful of your remaining EN.
Be wary of the enemy's range of attack.
Try using support skills.
Try using character skills.
Keep an eye on your Vigor.
Try using MAP Weapons.
Formations: mind terrain capability.
Formations: consider types.
Formations: consider attack traits.
Formations: utilize leader skills.
Give unit limit-breaking a try.
Give supporter limit-breaking a try.
Try equipping modifications.

As you level up your weapons, their Power increases while their EN
consumption decreases. Collect weapon upgrade material to enhance your weapons.

When you destroy an enemy with an attack, you can take another action
during the same turn. Therefore, think carefully about which units to use to
finish off enemies.

When another one of your units attacks, they will be able to perform an
additional attack. This action is only possible for characters
with Support Attack.

When your units or guest force units are attacked, characters with
Support Defense can take damage in place of the attacked unit.

After performing SP conversion on a unit, its base stats increase and it can
be leveled up to 100.

Characters can be recruited from "Scout." You can access "Scout" by going to
"Upgrade," then "Character."

Acquired mobile suits can be dismantled. You can obtain G-Shop Pts and rare
medals through dismantlement.

Some Development Diagrams allow you to obtain Ultimate Units upon completion.
Ultimate Units have their own Development Diagrams and require special
materials for development.

Some battles will let you unlock secret battles upon meeting certain
conditions. Clearing secret battles will net you valuable development
materials.

Save your frequently used formations. You will then be able to load them when
sortieing.

As time passes, you will be able to acquire items from the "Warship Cruise"
at your Base. Be sure to check your Base regularly.

You can accept requests from "Character Requests." Completing requests
improves your rank, allowing you then to acquire development materials and
Modifications.

EN is consumed whenever you use weapons. It recovers gradually each turn.
You cannot activate your weapons if you do not have enough EN, so always
keep an eye on your remaining EN.

Areas marked with "!" indicate the enemy's counter range. As long as you stay
outside of this area, you can attack the enemy without suffering any
counterattack.

Supporters possess support skills. As some of these can restore HP and EN to
units, determining the best time to use them is key.

Characters have character skills. These provide various effects, so choose
the best time to use them according to your strategy.

Vigor increases with MP, which in turn increases the damage you deal.
Build up MP by landing attacks and performing Support Attack and
Support Defense.

MAP Weapons are powerful weapons that can attack multiple enemies
simultaneously. They can only be activated before moving, so pay attention
to the units' positions when using them.

Stages have different terrains. If units do not have suitable terrain
capabilities, they will be restricted when sortieing or moving.

Units and characters belong to one of three types: attack, durability, or
support. By matching types when making formations, you can enhance the
effects of their abilities.

Weapons have weapon attributes, such as beam and physical. Certain attacks
will deal less damage to certain enemies, so be sure to use them judiciously.

Supporters have leader skills that enhance your entire formation. Put units
into your formation that have tags or are from series that leader skills
apply to.

Performing limit breaks on units greatly increases their stats.
Limit breaking may also unlock a home screen animation for some UR units.

Performing limit breaks on supporters enhances their leader skills.
Limit breaks require either duplicate supporters or supporter limit break materials.

Units can be equipped with Modifications. Modifications are also referred to
as mods and mainly grant effects that alter stats.
